content.text: are techniques for managing Claude's context window when it approaches capacity. They involve
older conversation history to make room for new tokens while preserving critical information.
— either oldest-first, selective content, or based on user-defined markers.
Responses get shorter and less specific
Claude asks you to re-explain things already covered
) vs. context window limits that cause content disappearance (fix: prune/summarize history).
Loses all context including reasoning behind decisions
Can't natively undo; loses early problem-discovery context
Lossy; Claude must regenerate lost details
Requires manual file management; no automatic sync
Token Consumption Layers in Claude Code

┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ System prompt (CLAUDE.md)  ~2k   │
│ Project context (settings) ~1k   │
│ Conversation history       fills │
│ Tool call results          large │
│ File contents (read_file)  large │
│ Available: 200k total              │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
), long conversation threads, repeated file reads.

Is context >70%?
  ├─ Yes → Need new phase? → /clear (with checkpoint summary)
  ├─ Yes → Continuing same work? → /compact with preservation
  ├─ No → Using targeted reads? → Keep going
  └─ No → Session ending? → Externalize to SESSION-NOTES.md

Save it to PROGRESS.md

Perfect control over exact content to keep; not elegant but effective.
/compact "Keep only the final plan and the last user prompt. Discard everything else."

/compact "MUST PRESERVE:
1. Database schema (users, orders, payments)
2. 4 API endpoints (POST /orders, GET /orders/:id, PATCH, DELETE)
3. Last test results (3 passed, 1 failed)
REMOVE: all code explorations, file reads, failed attempts"
Would keep only last 5 message pairs —
Keep everything from specific message onward.
Allow users to set checkpoint markers; prune from checkpoint onward.

# Quit session
aichat resume <session-id>

# From TUI select:
✂ Trim: clones session, truncates long tool calls/results + older messages → 50% usage
🔗 Rollover: new session + injects session log file path → 15–20% usage

uv tool install claude-code-tools
claude plugin marketplace add pchalasani/claude-code-tools
claude plugin install "aichat@cctools-plugins"

## Session Handoff Protocol

- What was accomplished
- What was NOT done (and why)
- Current blockers
- Next session starting point
- Any important context future sessions need

# Session at 160k tokens (80%)
aichat resume session-12345

# Select Trim option
# Result: 160k → 80k tokens (50% usage)
Truncates long tool call results + older assistant messages.

aichat resume session-12345

# Select Rollover option
# Creates new session with session log file path injected
# Result: 160k → 24k tokens (15% usage)
Claude uses sub-agent to recover context from original session log file.

:
- Without truncation strategy: 145,000 tokens, 3 compactions, 72% accuracy, 38 min, $0.44/session
- With rollover + targeted reads: 52,000 tokens, 0 compactions, 94% accuracy, 14 min, $0.16/session
-
between major feature phases; externalize session notes to
Compaction only summarizes conversational context, not files
End every long session with SESSION-NOTES.md
: Timestamped JSON snapshots for rollback before each compaction
: aichat preserves original logs for recovery via rollover
: SESSION-NOTES.md provides audit trail for production workflows
: Truncation and compaction are irreversible — use external backups
Truncation drops messages; compaction summarizes them
before acting: shorter responses, hedging, truncated outputs
For your full-stack distributed systems work at Box Hill, I'd prioritize:
checkpoint summaries + aichat rollover + targeted reads + SESSION-NOTES.md externalization
. This gives you 85–99% token reduction with 94–98% accuracy preservation.
